attempt_summary: {'dispatch_policy': 'Run the electrolyzer at 12 MW in every hour; use renewables first and buy grid power only when needed to top up to full load, since all grid prices are below the break-even threshold.', 'minimum_load_MW': 3, 'break_even_grid_price_usd_per_MWh': 111.53846153846153, 'total_hydrogen_output_kg': 1384.6153846153845, 'total_electricity_cost_usd': 1374, 'total_revenue_usd': 8030.76923076923, 'total_gross_margin_usd': 6656.76923076923, 'total_curtailment_MWh': 2}

rubric_item_scores.improvement_suggestion: For maximum compatibility with automated verifiers, consider using the exact expected header names (hour_index, renewable_available_MW, electrolyzer_power_used_MW, etc.) or explicitly include a one-line header mapping in operations_note.md stating the alternative column names used.
rubric_item_scores.notes: Both required artifacts are present and parseable. The CSV is comma-separated and parsed successfully. operations_note.md documents CSV column meanings and the mapping between the provided column names and the expected variables, allowing reproducible parsing despite header name differences.
rubric_item_scores.reasoning_summary: Both required artifacts are present and parseable. The CSV is comma-separated and parsed successfully. operations_note.md documents CSV column meanings and the mapping between the provided column names and the expected variables, allowing reproducible parsing despite header name differences.

rubric_item_scores.improvement_suggestion: None required for rule enforcement; consider explicitly adding a short per-hour justification line in operations_note.md linking each hour's grid purchase decision to the computed per-hour margin to make automated checks trivial.
rubric_item_scores.notes: Per-hour dispatch shows renewable-first allocation (renewable_used_MW = min(renewable, dispatch)), grid purchases only to top up to 12 MW, and the electrolyzer never runs below minimum load (3 MW). operations_note.md computes the break-even grid price (~$111.54/MWh) and shows that all hourly grid prices are below that threshold, justifying grid purchases. No undocumented below-minimum operations or renewable curtailment without explanation were found.
rubric_item_scores.reasoning_summary: Per-hour dispatch shows renewable-first allocation (renewable_used_MW = min(renewable, dispatch)), grid purchases only to top up to 12 MW, and the electrolyzer never runs below minimum load (3 MW). operations_note.md computes the break-even grid price (~$111.54/MWh) and shows that all hourly grid prices are below that threshold, justifying grid purchases. No undocumented below-minimum operations or renewable curtailment without explanation were found.

rubric_item_scores.improvement_suggestion: Include an explicit column or line in operations_note.md showing electricity_consumed_kWh (or the conversion used) to match every verifier expectation; although values are reproducible, adding the explicit kWh column improves clarity for reviewers who expect the exact CSV column names.
rubric_item_scores.notes: Arithmetic and unit conversions are consistent and reproducible from the provided inputs. Hydrogen production uses 1000/52 kg per MWh and yields 230.769231 kg/h at 12 MW. Electricity costs are computed as grid_purchased_MW * grid_price ($/MWh) for 1 hour, revenues as kg * $5.80, and gross margins equal revenue minus electricity cost. Cumulative totals match per-hour sums.
